laws.title: Public Law 1-1 | Enactment of the U.S. Codes Act
laws.status_basis: Foundational enactment establishing the U.S. Code baseline; no source text in the reviewed materials shows a wholesale repeal.

laws.title: Public Law 1-2 | United States Bar Act
laws.status_basis: The archive classifies this enactment as repealed_recorded, so the bar-exam rule should be preserved as history rather than current law.

laws.title: Public Law 1-3 | Closing Cabinet Doors Act
laws.status_basis: The act permanently restructures executive departments and contains no self-repeal or sunset; the blanket repeal language belongs in history-note treatment, not section-level strikes.

laws.title: Public Law 1-4 | Retracting of Resignations Act
laws.status_basis: The archive records this enactment as repealed_recorded; the section-3(a) defect affects codification only, not the fact that the law is history.

laws.title: Public Law 2-5 | Judicial Efficiency Act (Keywords: Maximum, Minimum, Sentence, Sentencing)
laws.status_basis: Public Law 19-149 expressly repealed this act in full and replaced its sentencing scheme.

laws.title: Public Law 2-6 | Shelby-Kabob Act of 2021
laws.status_basis: Public Law 14-89 later repealed this act in full and enacted replacement misconduct rules.

laws.title: Public Law 2-7 | Court of Appeals Temporary Suspension Act of 2021
laws.status_basis: The act is expressly superseded and repealed in full by later law PL 3-24.

laws.title: Public Law 2-8 | National Security Act of 2021
laws.status_basis: Later law PL 3-25 repealed this act and replaced the national-security framework with revised provisions.

laws.title: Public Law 2-9 | District of Columbia City Charter
laws.status_basis: Public Law 36-260 dissolves the municipal government and states that any inconsistent District law, ordinance, or regulation 'shall be repealed', which leaves this charter only as history.

laws.title: Public Law 2-10 | Amendment to the District of Columbia City Charter
laws.status_basis: Public Law 36-260 dissolves the municipal government and states that any inconsistent District law, ordinance, or regulation 'shall be repealed', so this charter amendment is historical only.

laws.title: Public Law 2-11 | Criminal Statute of Limitations Act
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition report classifies this law as repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED with high confidence, so the law is not current despite the substantive limitations rule in the source text.

laws.title: Public Law 2-12 | Fee Prohibition Act
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition report classifies this law as repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED with high confidence.

laws.title: Public Law 2-13 | The Employees Termination Notification Act of 2021
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition report classifies this law as repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED with high confidence.

laws.title: Public Law 2-14 | National Federal Torts Act
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition report classifies this law as repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED with high confidence.

laws.title: Public Law 3-15 | Admission to the United Nations Act
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition report classifies this law as repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED with high confidence.

laws.title: Public Law 3-16 | The Capitol Police Establishment
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition report classifies this law as repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED with high confidence.

laws.title: Public Law 3-17 | The Diplomatic Protections Act
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition report classifies this law as repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED with high confidence.

laws.title: Public Law 3-18 | Amendment to S.12
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition report classifies this law as repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED with high confidence.

laws.title: Public Law 3-19 | The NARA Act
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition report classifies this law as active / STATUTORY_NOTE with high confidence, and the live XML note aligns with that disposition.

laws.title: Public Law 3-20 | National Immigration Employment Act of 2021
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition report classifies this source-limited law as repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED with high confidence, but the text body itself is unavailable because the PDF is scanned.

laws.title: Public Law 3-21 | Effective Judicial Function and General Duties Act
laws.status_basis: The enacted source is complete and reviewable, and the codification register places it as a statutory note at 28 U.S.C. § 1658. It remains active, but it does not warrant positive-law text in this batch.

laws.title: Public Law 3-22 | District of Columbia Administration Act
laws.status_basis: The supplied source is only Google Drive viewer debris and does not recover the enactment text. No lawful substantive conclusion can be drawn from the material provided.

laws.title: Public Law 3-23 | Federal Law Enforcement Training Center Establishment Act
laws.status_basis: The metadata marks the source unrecoverable and the recovered stub says the source was deleted or not found. Because no enactment text is available, the operative provisions cannot be reconstructed lawfully.

laws.title: Public Law 3-24 | Judgeship Reducation Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: The codification register records this law as repealed/nonoperative with no present code location. The source text is substantive judicial reorganization history only, and the batch should not treat it as live law.

laws.title: Public Law 3-25 | CRACK Act
laws.status_basis: Later replacement laws fully displaced the act: PL 38-263 repeals Titles I through IV, and PL 38-264 repeals Titles V and VI and the amendments made by those titles. No live CRACK framework remains to codify.

laws.title: Public Law 4-26 | H.J. Res. 06 (Keywords: Tigray)
laws.status_basis: The source is a resolution that declares a state of war and contains no self-repeal or sunset. It is non-code in character but not expired on its face.

laws.title: Public Law 4-27 | Right to Security Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: Later law PL 29-217 expressly says that Public Law 4-27 shall be repealed in its entirety. The source act was substantive private-security and self-defense law, but it now survives only as history.

laws.title: Public Law 4-28 | House Veterans Affairs Act
laws.status_basis: The codification register records this law as nonoperative/repealed with no code location. The veterans-policy text survives only as history in this batch.

laws.title: Public Law 4-29 | Department of Commerce and Labor Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: Later law PL 31-226 expressly says that Public Law 4-29 shall be repealed in its entirety. The source act established a new executive department and transfer framework, but it no longer remains in force.

laws.title: Public Law 4-30 | Fugitive Justice Reform Act
laws.status_basis: Later law PL 29-207 repeals Public Law 4-30. The source act created a fugitive-list and warrant-database regime, but the operative enactment has been displaced and survives only as repeal history.

laws.title: Public Law 4-31 | Stand Your Ground Act
laws.status_basis: The archive classifies the enactment as a repealed-law item, and the current record treats it as history only. The source text is a substantive self-defense statute, but it does not remain live positive law in the current archive posture.

laws.title: Public Law 4-32 | Warrants, Subpoenas, and Summons Act
laws.status_basis: The recovered source is an executive order that merely cites Public Law 4-32 as authority; the enacted statute text itself is not recoverable from the supplied source. No lawful codification conclusion can be drawn for the act from that file alone.

laws.title: Public Law 4-33 | National Archives Act
laws.status_basis: Listed in the source manifest under Repealed Laws. Internally the act establishes NARA and archival responsibilities, but no self-repeal or sunset appears in the source text; repeal is external, so the law should be retained only as history.

laws.title: Public Law 4-34 | An Act to Prevent the Proliferation of Magistrates and Ensure Basic Judicial Competence
laws.status_basis: Listed in the source manifest under Repealed Laws. The source creates a permanent general bar-certification rule for magistrate and judicial appointments, but no self-repeal or sunset appears internally; repeal is external.

laws.title: Public Law 4-35 | Recommissioning of the United States Ship New Jersey Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: The source is a Google Drive permission-denied page, so no statutory text is recoverable and no lawful codification conclusion can be drawn.

laws.title: Public Law 4-36 | Defense Heraldic Services Act
laws.status_basis: Listed under 1st - 10th U.S. Congress, not repealed. The rendered HTML contains the operative text: it repeals 10 U.S.C. 7594 and adds a new heraldic-services section in chapter 48, subchapter II of title 10.

laws.title: Public Law 4-37 | Border Awareness and Naturalization Act
laws.status_basis: Listed in the source manifest under Repealed Laws. The source creates a permanent general immigration and naturalization framework, but no self-repeal or sunset appears internally; repeal is external.

laws.title: Public Law 4-38 | White House Protection Act
laws.status_basis: Listed in the source manifest under Repealed Laws. The source creates a permanent general Secret Service jurisdiction and weapons rule around the White House, but no self-repeal or sunset appears internally; repeal is external.

laws.title: Public Law 7-39 | Mandatory FLETC Training ACT
laws.status_basis: Expressly repealed by Public Law 9-57, which states that Public Law 7-39 is repealed and the provisions of law amended or repealed by it are restored or revived as if the act had not been enacted. The law is also listed under Repealed Laws.

laws.title: Public Law 7-40 | Media Companies Protection Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: Expressly repealed by Public Law 9-53, which states that Public Law 7-40 is repealed. The law is also listed under Repealed Laws.

laws.title: Public Law 7-41 | Common Sense Defense Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: Listed under Repealed Laws. The source is a template-style draft with game-specific firearm and pricing provisions, and no self-repeal or sunset appears internally; repeal is external.

laws.title: Public Law 7-42 | Transportation Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: Listed under 'Repealed Laws'. Internally permanent general (creates transit divisions, FTA, TSA); no self-repeal/sunset (contains a repeal of another measure, not itself). Repeal external.

laws.title: Public Law 7-43 | District of Columbia Reorganization Initiative Act
laws.status_basis: Listed under 'Repealed Laws'. Purely local/District measure amending the D.C. City Charter; no self-repeal/sunset. Repeal external.

laws.title: Public Law 7-44 | The Bye Bye Terrorism Act
laws.status_basis: Listed under 'Repealed Laws'. Internally permanent general (limits fully-automatic firearms to semi-auto); no self-repeal/sunset. Repeal external.

laws.title: Public Law 7-45 | United States Capitol Police Reorganization Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: Listed under 'Repealed Laws'. Internally reorganizes Capitol Police (repeals parts of a prior 'S. 12'); no self-repeal/sunset of itself. Repeal external. Note PL 9-53 SEC. 5 later authorizes transfer/suspension of Capitol Police authority, and PL 9-60 makes the MPD Chief an ex-officio Capitol Police Board member â€” related but not repealing.

laws.title: Public Law 7-46 | Whistleblower Protection Act
laws.status_basis: Source unrecoverable: law.txt is Google-Drive access-denied debris ('Sorry, the owner hasn't given you permission to download this file'). No statutory text available; no lawful codification conclusion possible. Listed under 'Repealed Laws' (external label only).

laws.title: Public Law 7-47 | The Federal Bar Association Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: Listed under 'Repealed Laws'. Internally reorganizes the Federal Bar Association (repeals a prior 'Senate Bill 2'); no self-repeal/sunset. Repeal external.

laws.title: Public Law 7-48 | OCJCS Reform Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: Listed under 'Repealed Laws'. Internally permanent general (reorganizes Joint Chiefs / combatant commands); no self-repeal/sunset. Repeal external.

laws.title: Public Law 7-49 | Legislative Initiative to Grow Monetary Access Act of 2022 (LIGMA Act)
laws.status_basis: Listed under 'Repealed Laws'. Internally grants recurring in-game compensation to legislators (appropriation-like/ongoing pay); no self-repeal but duration clause makes it ongoing. Repeal external.

laws.title: Public Law 7-50 | Coast Guard Base Repurposing Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: Listed under 'Repealed Laws'. Internally a one-time property-repurposing directive for a specific installation; no self-repeal/sunset. Repeal external.

laws.title: Public Law 7-51 | Federal Agent Law Training Mandate
laws.status_basis: The source is unrecoverable, and chronology does not justify inventing executable text; the law stays as a historical note only.

laws.title: Public Law 9-52 | The Federal District Wages Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: The canonical Trello record places this law in Repealed Laws. The mass-migration disposition report marks the source unavailable, so the enactment text cannot be reconstructed and the record can remain only as a source-limited historical note.

laws.title: Public Law 9-53 | Save Our Businesses Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: The law is already incorporated in the current codification state at 3 U.S.C. section 301, and the codification decision for this law found no express later repeal or replacement of that amendment. The earlier repeal label on the Trello card does not displace the already-incorporated Code text.

laws.title: Public Law 9-54 | The Smart Label Congress Act
laws.status_basis: The canonical Trello record places this law in Repealed Laws. The mass-migration disposition report marks the source unavailable, so the enactment text cannot be reconstructed and the record can remain only as a source-limited historical note.

laws.title: Public Law 9-55 | Economic Sanctions on Iran Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: The source text is available and shows a sanctions framework, but the law is recorded as repealed in the codification inventory and does not remain current positive law. It survives only as repeal history.

laws.title: Public Law 9-56 | Federal Judicial Reform Act
laws.status_basis: The source text is available and contains a complete judiciary overhaul, but the law is recorded as repealed in the codification inventory and is not current positive law. It should remain history only.

laws.title: Public Law 9-57 | Repealing S.B.1/Pub. Law 7-39
laws.status_basis: The mass-migration disposition report marks the law repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED, and the later RESET Act repeal list also names Public Law 9-57. This law now survives only as history.

laws.title: Public Law 9-58 | To Bar PhillipRobecq From Public Office
laws.status_basis: This is a private, person-specific barment measure and the codification inventory records it as repealed. It is inherently not permanent general law and belongs in history only.

laws.title: Public Law 9-59 | M.P.D Act
laws.status_basis: The act is a local District of Columbia policing measure and is recorded as repealed. It is superseded by Public Law 9-60 and now survives only as repeal history.

laws.title: Public Law 9-60 | The Federal Metro Policing Act
laws.status_basis: The mass-migration disposition report marks the act repealed / NONOPERATIVE_OR_REPEALED and no current Code text survives. It also operates as the later repealer of Public Law 9-59, but it now belongs in history only.

laws.title: Public Law 9-61 | Charter Repeal Act
laws.status_basis: The local District of Columbia charter repeal is recorded as repealed and the review context notes that later Public Law 12-84 expressly repeals H.R. 7. The law now survives only as history.

laws.title: Public Law 9-62 | Repealing the District of Columbia Home Rule Act
laws.status_basis: Operative local/District repeal act; it repeals the D.C. Home Rule Act and does not create a new positive-law section or self-repeal.

laws.title: Public Law 9-63 | The Federal District Employment Act of 2022
laws.status_basis: The source is unrecoverable, so no operative statutory text can be authenticated from the record provided.

laws.title: Public Law 9-64 | To Bar T800Ninja123 From Public Office
laws.status_basis: Operative individual barment resolution; it takes effect by its terms and does not self-repeal or expire.

laws.title: Public Law 10-65 | Federal Judicial Reform Act ii
laws.status_basis: The supplied source is a court opinion, not enacted statutory text, and it declares the challenged sections unconstitutional and null and void.

laws.title: Public Law 10-66 | The Americans First Act
laws.status_basis: Operative repeal act; it repeals prior laws on its own terms and does not self-repeal or expire.

laws.title: Public Law 10-67 | Ambassadorial Readjustment Act
laws.status_basis: General foreign-affairs organizational legislation; the source creates permanent ambassadorial rules and does not self-repeal or expire.

laws.title: Public Law 10-68 | American Jobs Act
laws.status_basis: Operative repeal act; the repeal of prior law takes effect on passage and the law does not self-repeal.

laws.title: Public Law 11-69 | DC Criminal Code Act
laws.status_basis: Comprehensive local/District criminal code; the source creates enforceable D.C. offenses and traffic rules and does not self-repeal or expire.

laws.title: Public Law 11-70 | Supreme Court Please Act
laws.status_basis: Operative transfer statute; it transfers Supreme Court Police duties to the U.S. Marshals Service and does not self-repeal or expire.

laws.title: Public Law 11-71 | Supreme Court Needs Act
laws.status_basis: Section 2(a) repeals 28 U.S.C. section 1 and section 2(b) immediately replaces it with new text setting the Court at a Chief Justice plus four associate justices. The source contains no self-repeal or sunset.

laws.title: Public Law 11-72 | No More Mariah Act (Keywords: Judges, Justices, Circuit Court)
laws.status_basis: Section 2 repeals Public Law 10-65 and section 3 replaces 28 U.S.C. section 133 with new District of Columbia judgeship text. The source itself does not self-repeal.

laws.title: Public Law 11-73 | Let Me Act
laws.status_basis: The review record classifies the law as repealed based on the Trello record, and no current Code operation is authorized.

laws.title: Public Law 12-74 | Federal Bar Association Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: Sections 4-5 establish and govern the Federal Bar Association as a new executive-branch independent establishment, and no later repeal of the act was identified.

laws.title: Public Law 12-75 | Freedom of Information Common Sense Act
laws.status_basis: Section 3 expressly replaces text in 5 U.S.C. section 552, so the act is a live Code amendment.

laws.title: Public Law 12-76 | Removing Idiocy Act
laws.status_basis: The manifest marks the source as unrecoverable because access was revoked or denied and no statutory text was extracted.

laws.title: Public Law 12-77 | Preventing Abuse and Promoting Democracy Act
laws.status_basis: Chronology ends with a repealed source-limited note; no executable Code text survives.

laws.title: Public Law 12-78 | Balancing Executive Act
laws.status_basis: The chronology remains historical only; the note should stay repealed and non-executable.

laws.title: Public Law 12-79 | Anti-Terrorism Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The record is source-limited, but the review classifies the enactment as current statutory-note material pending text recovery; no contrary repeal was identified.

laws.title: Public Law 12-80 | Government Labor Management Act
laws.status_basis: PL-031-226 repeals Public Law 12-80 in its entirety, so no current Code insertion is authorized.

laws.title: Public Law 12-81 | The Integrity Act
laws.status_basis: The act is a general and permanent enactment, but the canonical report treats it as a statutory note at 2 U.S.C. section 6567.

laws.title: Public Law 12-82 | Military Officer Commissioning Reform Act 2023
laws.status_basis: The source text directly amends Title 10 sections 531, 12203, 12204, and 151 and does not self-repeal or sunset.

laws.title: Public Law 12-83 | Dannybec Went to Jail Act
laws.status_basis: The source expressly repeals Public Law 7-42 and directs DOT shutdown, but it does not repeal itself or sunset.

laws.title: Public Law 12-84 | Municipal Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: This is a local/District governance enactment. It does not self-repeal; it is operational local law, not permanent federal Code text.

laws.title: Public Law 12-85 | The Congress Strikes Back Act
laws.status_basis: The source is an individual-specific barment enactment. It is operative law, but it is not suitable for permanent positive-law codification.

laws.title: Public Law 12-86 | H.R. 1302
laws.status_basis: The source repeals a section of the New District of Columbia Home Rule Act. It is operative local repeal legislation and does not self-repeal.

laws.title: Public Law 12-87 | An act to repeal the Smart Label Congress Act (Public Law 9-54)
laws.status_basis: The source expressly nullifies Public Law 9-54 and sets chamber administration rules, but it does not repeal itself.

laws.title: Public Law 12-88 | New District of Columbia Home Rule Act
laws.status_basis: This is a comprehensive local/District home-rule enactment. It remains operative as local law and does not self-repeal.

laws.title: Public Law 12-103 | Ceremonial 5-Star Officer Creation Act
laws.status_basis: The source creates a permanent O-11 military-grade rule and does not self-repeal.

laws.title: Public Law 14-89 | Americans Safety and Smart Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The source repeals Public Law 2-6 and creates new criminal offenses, but it does not self-repeal.

laws.title: Public Law 14-90 | Presidential Suck Up Act
laws.status_basis: The source creates a general acting-authority waiver for nominated officials and does not self-repeal.

laws.title: Public Law 14-91 | Military Code of Military Justice Reform Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The source is a shell/proposed-UCMJ text; it does not supply the substantive military-code rewrite needed to fix a lawful Code disposition.

laws.title: Public Law 15-92 | Commerce and Labor Innovation Initiative Act
laws.status_basis: PL 31-226 repeals this law in its entirety before codification, so no current Code insertion survives.

laws.title: Public Law 15-93 | Checks and Balances Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The act is an operative repealer; nothing in the supplied materials repeals PL 15-93 itself, so it remains current as repeal-history legislation.

laws.title: Public Law 15-94 | Whistleblower Protections Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The PDF is image-only/scanned and no recoverable enactment text is available, so the operative statutory content cannot be verified.

laws.title: Public Law 15-95 | An Act to Repeal the White House Protection Act
laws.status_basis: The source is unrecoverable, so the repeal target and scope cannot be authenticated.

laws.title: Public Law 15-96 | Prisoner Bail Protection Act
laws.status_basis: PL 16-113 expressly repeals PL 15-96 in full, so the law belongs in history only.

laws.title: Public Law 15-97 | Uniform Legal Practice Authority Act
laws.status_basis: Title I was later repealed in full, while Title II remains operative and PL 15-105 also partially amended Title I before that repeal.

laws.title: Public Law 15-98 | Inspector General Act (2023)
laws.status_basis: The enactment is classified as commemorative/non-Code material, so it remains a public law but does not warrant a U.S. Code insertion.

laws.title: Public Law 15-99 | Second National Federal Torts Act
laws.status_basis: The act is a general and permanent tort statute, and nothing in the supplied materials repeals it; it belongs in the Title 28 note chain.

laws.title: Public Law 15-100 | Employee Rights and Limitations Act (leave, employment, termination)
laws.status_basis: The source text is substantive and permanent, and later amendment PL 17-131 confirms that it remains live rather than repealed.

laws.title: Public Law 15-101 | Ensuring Accountability in Federal Agencies Act
laws.status_basis: The recovered source text is the Guidance to Guidances Act: it creates agency guidance-publication rules, a FOIA carve-out, and related reporting mechanics. No repeal or sunset appears in the source or later-law materials reviewed.

laws.title: Public Law 15-102 | New Ambassadorial Organizational and Reporting Act
laws.status_basis: The source remains operative. It expressly repeals the Ambassadorial Readjustment Act, repeals PL 14-90 section 2(a)(i), creates regional ambassadorships, and imposes weekly reporting duties.

laws.title: Public Law 15-104 | The Military Justice Reorganization and Reform Act
laws.status_basis: Public Law 29-210 expressly repeals PL 15-104 and PL 19-146 and states that the repeals do not revive earlier Code text. The later-law decision leaves no present XML operation safely determinable.

laws.title: Public Law 15-105 | Municipal Prosecution Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The source is operative and continues to govern the District of Columbia prosecution structure. It contains express repeals and substitutions affecting earlier District-law provisions, but no later law in the reviewed materials repeals PL 15-105 itself.

laws.title: Public Law 15-106 | Congress Strikes Back Repealment
laws.status_basis: The act is a live repealing statute. Its substantive effect is to repeal The Congress Strikes Back Act, while the repealing statute itself remains current.

laws.title: Public Law 15-107 | Guidance to Guidances Act
laws.status_basis: The source is unrecoverable. No authenticated enactment text could be reconstructed, and the title appears to duplicate PL 15-101's recovered source text. The law should not be invented from the gap.

laws.title: Public Law 15-108 | Reclassification of Labeling Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The recovered source sets internal congressional document-labeling rules. It is substantive public-law text, but it is not positive-law Code text and should be treated as note material or excluded from permanent Code sections.

laws.title: Public Law 15-109 | Ending Tigray for the Last Time
laws.status_basis: The source is a current joint resolution whose substantive effect is to repeal House Joint Resolution 6. It is the repealing instrument, not the repealed object.

laws.title: Public Law 15-110 | A Bill to Direct the Inspector General of the Department of Defense to Conduct an Investigation Into the Attack on the United States Embassy in Damascus, and for Other Purposes
laws.status_basis: The source is unrecoverable. No authenticated statutory text could be verified, and the title suggests a one-time investigative directive that would not normally warrant permanent Code text even if recovered.

laws.title: Public Law 15-111 | Presidential Transition Protection Act
laws.status_basis: The recovered source creates a criminal prohibition on obstructing a presidential transition, defines the key terms, and sets a short jail-and-fine penalty. No later law in the reviewed materials repeals it.

laws.title: Public Law 16-112 | The Contempt of Congress Act
laws.status_basis: Chronology resolves this law as superseded: Pub. L. 31-228 later repealed Public Law 16-112 and reinstated 2 U.S.C. 192 in full. The recovered source bundle is an access-denied page, so no operative text can be reconstructed.

laws.title: Public Law 16-113 | The Bing Bong Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and operative. It repeals Public Law 15-96 in full and has no self-repeal or sunset, so the repealing statute itself remains current.

laws.title: Public Law 16-114 | Amended Ambassadorial Organization and Reporting Act
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and operative. Its only substantive effect is an express amendment to PL 15-102 SEC III(e) with determinable strike-and-insert text, and nothing repeals the act itself.

laws.title: Public Law 16-115 | National Security Threat Mandatory Notification to Congressional Leadership Act
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and fully operative. It creates a notification duty for foreign threats, defines terms, and ties noncompliance to 18 U.S.C. 1512. Nothing repeals it.

laws.title: Public Law 16-116 | Combatant Command Re-establishment Act
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and operative. It repeals the OCJCS Reform Act of 2022 / PL 7-48 and re-establishes six Unified Combatant Commands with command-and-control rules. Nothing repeals PL 16-116.

laws.title: Public Law 16-117 | The Clean Up Act
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and operative. It repeals Senate Joint Resolution 2 and 3; the repealing resolution itself remains current.

laws.title: Public Law 16-118 | The Uniformed Capitol Police Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The authoritative source text is readable and operative. It repeals H.R. 8013 Section III and replaces that section with a new Capitol Police Board structure and powers. Nothing repeals PL 16-118.

laws.title: Public Law 16-119 | The Criminal Organization Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The source bundle is an access-denied page, so no operative text can be authenticated. Claude's chronology keeps the law current but source-limited rather than executable.

laws.title: Public Law 16-120 | The Hail Columbia Act
laws.status_basis: Claude's chronology keeps this as historical-note material only; the source bundle is an access-denied page, so no executable Code text survives.

laws.title: Public Law 16-121 | The Bill Transparency Understanding Act
laws.status_basis: Claude's chronology keeps this as a repealed historical note; the source bundle is an access-denied page, so no executable Code text survives.

laws.title: Public Law 16-122 | The Bill Transparency Understanding Act
laws.status_basis: The supplied enactment text is unrecoverable. The source file is only a Google Drive permission-denial page, so no operative language can be authenticated or codified.

laws.title: Public Law 17-124 | Law Enforcement Accountability Act (LEAA)
laws.status_basis: The full source is present and the act is a substantive, generally applicable law-enforcement statute. It does not repeal itself, and no later repeal appears in the supplied materials.

laws.title: Public Law 17-125 | We Don't Read Minds Act of 2023
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and operative. It imposes monthly Inspector General reporting duties and penalties for noncompliance, and the supplied materials do not show a repeal of the act itself.

laws.title: Public Law 17-126 | Special Events Act
laws.status_basis: The authoritative disposition trail classifies this enactment as superseded before codification, with PL-031-226 later repealing Public Law 17-126 in full. The current XML note is therefore a historical artifact, not live Code text.

laws.title: Public Law 17-127 | Border Security Act
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and contains a blanket Title 8 repeal followed by multiple express rewrites of Title 8 sections. It remains operative in the source chain and is not itself repealed.

laws.title: Public Law 17-128 | Impeachment Procedures and Protocols Act (IPPA)
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and contains congressional-procedure rules plus a determinable amendment to 28 U.S. Code 1365. It is not self-repealed, and the supplied materials do not show a later repeal.

laws.title: Public Law 17-129 | Department of Education Act
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and supplies an organic-act style Department of Education framework. It is a live enactment, not a repeal, and its substantive provisions are permanent.

laws.title: Public Law 17-130 | Oath and Offices Protection (OOP)
laws.status_basis: The source is readable and creates a substantive oath-timing rule tied to 5 U.S. Code 3331. It does not repeal itself and should not be treated as Title 50 material.

laws.title: Public Law 17-131 | More Jobs Amendment

laws.title: Public Law 19-149 | Criminal Justice Reform Act (CJRA)

laws.title: Public Law 19-150 | Small Businesses Empowerment Act

laws.title: Public Law 22-163 | Employee's Bill of Rights

laws.title: Public Law 23-169 | Military Readiness Act

laws.status_basis: The source is a live appropriations-style enactment: it authorizes issuance of fifty million dollars, sets spending limits and a reserved emergency fund, and contains an immediate effective date. Nothing in the text indicates repeal or expiration.

laws.title: Public Law 35-256 | Workers First Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The source reduces the income tax rate from 28 percent to 23 percent and then supplies supporting limitations and an immediate effective date. The text is operative, but it does not name a determinable U.S. Code target in the accessible source.

laws.title: Public Law 36-256 | Commerce Dispersal Act
laws.status_basis: The source dissolves the Department of Commerce and Labor, redistributes its functions, and transfers copyright administration. It is an operative structural reorganization, not a repealed enactment.

laws.title: Public Law 36-257 | Maritime Operations & Policy Overhaul Act
laws.status_basis: The source amends prior public laws governing military and Coast Guard policy, creates contingent Coast Guard authorities, and adds maritime-security coordination. It is operative and not shown as repealed.

laws.title: Public Law 36-258 | National Root Beer Day Act
laws.status_basis: The source is an active amendment to title 5: it inserts National Root Beer Day into the federal holiday list and includes an immediate enactment clause. No repeal or supersession appears in the materials reviewed.

laws.title: Public Law 36-259 | Run Me My Money Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The source is a supplemental appropriations bill that allocates money to specific departments and agencies, reserves an emergency fund, and takes effect immediately. It is still operative law.

laws.title: Public Law 36-260 | Fixing the District of Columbia Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The source expressly repeals six prior public laws, dissolves the District municipal government, transfers MPD and DCFEMS, reestablishes the D.C. Delegate, adopts municipal laws into federal law, and amends Public Law 22-156. Nothing in the materials shows it to be repealed or superseded.

laws.title: Public Law 37-261 | Federal Criminal Code Act of 2025
laws.status_basis: The accessible source gives a short title, severability, an immediate effective date, and a section adopting an attached Federal Criminal Code. The act is operative, but the attachment text is not determinable from the accessible source alone.

laws.title: Public Law 38-262 | Fund the Government Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: Full text is present. The act is not self-repealing and includes both temporary appropriations and a permanent repeal/rewrite of prior schedule rules, so it remains operative rather than expired.

laws.title: Public Law 38-263 | National Security Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act creates an ongoing national-security eligibility framework, classification rules, and administrative-subpoena safeguards; it also repeals prior CRACK Act titles I-IV, but nothing in the source self-expires.

laws.title: Public Law 38-264 | Organized Crime and Racketeering Modernization Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act repeals CRACK Act titles V and VI and installs a continuing racketeering framework with penalties, civil remedies, and disqualification rules; it is not self-limiting or expired.

laws.title: Public Law 38-265 | Employment Clarity and Federal Workforce Modernization Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act expressly repeals earlier employment statutes and installs a continuing private- and federal-employment framework with review, anti-retaliation, and enforcement rules.

laws.title: Public Law 38-266 | Speedy Trial Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The source creates continuing speedy-trial rules and directly amends Public Law 4-32; nothing in the enactment self-terminates.

laws.title: Public Law 39-267 | Reform Sentencing Act
laws.status_basis: The source repeals PL 34-251 and replaces it with ongoing sentencing rules, enforcement, and multi-charge limits; it is not self-limiting or sunsetted.

laws.title: Public Law 40-268 | Big Beautiful Budget Act
laws.status_basis: This is a one-off appropriations act with short-form enactment language and no permanent statutory target; the appropriations and oversight provisions are time-bound rather than enduring Code text.

laws.title: Public Law 41-269 | Federal Enhancement Against Foreign Intrusion Amendments Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act makes continuing amendments to Public Law 24-173 and adds a new disclosure/enforcement section; nothing in the source sunsets or self-repeals.

laws.title: Public Law 41-270 | Washington Diplomacy Complex Corrections Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: The act directly revises the Washington Diplomacy Complex Act 2025 in multiple sections and adds technical corrections; it is an ongoing corrective amendment, not a repealed or expired law.

laws.title: Public Law 41-271 | The Great Change Act of 2026
laws.status_basis: This is a broad integrating act that repeals, amends, transfers, and preserves multiple existing laws while installing continuing reforms across security, commerce, ethics, military service, and whistleblower enforcement.
